channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Israel’s Netanyahu directs army to seize 70 percent of Gaza Strip
item.description: Israeli army has already expanded its control of Gaza by 11 percent over the 'Yellow Line', beyond terms of 'ceasefire'.

item.title: Rebuilding US weapons stockpile may ‘take years’ post-Iran war
item.description: Restoring pre-war stockpiles of critical US munitions will take at least two years, according to CSIS report.

item.title: US, Iran reach MoU on 60-day truce, Trump’s approval pending
item.description: US and Iranian negotiators have reached a memorandum of understanding on a proposed 60-day ceasefire extension.

item.title: US and Iran reach tentative deal for 60-day truce extension, officials say
item.description: The memorandum of understanding, which would enable further negotiations, still requires Donald Trump's final approval.

item.title: CNN sues Perplexity, alleging unlawful distribution of copyrighted content
item.description: Perplexity unlawfully copied thousands of CNN stories, videos and images to power its products, CNN said in its lawsuit.

item.title: How War Changed Eid in Lebanon
item.description: After nearly three years of war and displacement, Eid in Lebanon no longer feels like celebration for many families.

item.title: UN ‘adds Israel to blacklist’ for conflict-related sexual violence
item.description: Israeli ambassador to the UN says Tel Aviv will cut ties with UN chief Antonio Guterres over the upcoming report.

item.title: Latvia parliament approves new gov’t after drone dispute toppled coalition
item.description: Latvia's government reform ensures a pro-Western path as regional drone incidents heighten security concerns.

item.title: US inflation surges to three-year high amid tensions with Iran
item.description: Heightened petrol prices led the inflationary pressures, with prices that jumped 5.5 percent in April.

item.title: Smoke rises over Beirut after Israeli strike
item.description: Footage shows smoke billowing over south Beirut following an Israeli attack on the Lebanese capital.

item.title: Hundreds flee India crackdown on undocumented migrants
item.description: Hundreds flee India crackdown on undocumented migrants

item.title: Fire kills 16 students at Kenyan girls’ boarding school
item.description: At least 16 students were killed and dozens injured after a fire tore through the dormitory of a girls’ school in Kenya.

item.title: WHO chief heads to ‘hardest hit’ Ituri as DRC’s 17th Ebola outbreak spreads
item.description: DRC battles Bundibugyo strain as confirmed cases rise to 121, with 17 deaths and 246 suspected fatalities reported.

item.title: US, Mexico, Canada announce Ebola-related travel measures for World Cup
item.description: World Cup hosts agree steps to counter Ebola threat after WHO declares public health emergency ​of international concern

item.title: US Treasury threatens Oman with sanctions over Hormuz Strait
item.description: US official says Oman should know that Washington 'will aggressively target' actors that facilitate tolls in waterway.

item.title: Neymar set to miss Brazil World Cup opener due to calf injury
item.description: Neymar was injured playing for Santos in the Brazil domestic league, and is now confirmed to miss World Cup friendlies.

item.title: IOC chief Coventry stirs social media storm over athlete payment comments
item.description: Olympians slam the IOC chief and Zimbabwe's former medallist for saying athletes should not be paid at the Games.

item.title: Yemen’s former leader Abd-Rabbu Mansour Hadi dies in exile at 80
item.description: Hadi was the internationally recognised president of Yemen who led a fractured government mostly from exile.

item.title: Poisonous invasion: What is the ‘devil’s trumpet’ harming crops in Iraq?
item.description: The spread of datura is forcing the Iraqi Interior Ministry to urge farmers and citizens to report if spotted.

item.title: US attacks Bandar Abbas again: Why is the port so important for Iran?
item.description: Bandar Abbas, which the US has targeted multiple times, occupies a strategically sensitive position in Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Sinner shocked in French Open exit as Cerundolo recovers from two-sets down
item.description: World number one Jannick Sinner beaten by 56th-ranked Argentinian opponent Juan Manuel Cerundolo at Roland Garros.

item.title: Teenager Kouame beats Vallejo at French Open to match 23-year Nadal record
item.description: French teenager Moise Kouame, who became youngest Grand Slam match winner in first round, defeats Adolfo Daniel Vallejo.

item.title: Anti-Immigrant anger grows in South Africa as migrants forced onto streets
item.description: Foreign nationals in Durban say they’ve been forced from their homes and businesses.

item.title: South African president to face impeachment probe over ‘Farmgate’ scandal
item.description: Impeachment committee to hold first meeting next week as President Cyril Ramaphosa challenges findings in court.

item.title: Gold Rush: Did CIA agent steal $40m in gold bars via work expenses?
item.description: FBI says ex-CIA official David Rush repeatedly lied about his credentials in US government job applications.
